aurora 0.0.0.928
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Add an Alpha Channel to Colors

Source: R/addAlpha.R
addAlpha.Rd

Add transparency to colors.

Usage

addAlpha(col, alpha = 0.5)

Arguments

col

Vector of valid R colors.

alpha

Alpha transparency values between 0 and 1.

Value

Character vector of hexadecimal colors with alpha channel.

See also

adjustcolor, colToOpaque

Other color: colToOpaque(), colToRGB(), contrastColor(), darken(), findColor(), grayscale(), hcol(), hexToRGB(), lighten(), mixColors(), pal(), palNames(), setBackCol()

Examples


op <- par(no.readonly = TRUE)

addAlpha("yellow", 0.2)
#>      yellow 
#> "#FFFF0033" 
addAlpha(2, 0.5)   # red
#> [1] "#DF536B80"

canvas(3)
polygon(circle(x=c(-1,0,1), y=c(1,-1,1), radius=2), 
        col=addAlpha(2:4, 0.4))


x <- rnorm(15000)
par(mfrow=c(1,2))
plot(x, type="p", col="blue" )
plot(x, type="p", col=addAlpha("blue", .2), 
     main="Better insight with alpha channel" )


par(op)
